With no order after a month, the procurement-pending thesis is no longer a live edge; it is just waiting on a catalyst that isn't coming on this timeframe. Close it.
2024-09-06.exit_advice: Exit. The entry catalyst (WHO mpox PHEIC declaration) occurred before entry and is long priced. The procurement payoff I held for has not materialized in four weeks; this week is again pure generic healthcare/market noise with zero mpox/SIGA/TPOXX-specific catalyst. The reflexive trade has fully decayed — edge gone.

This week's news is a THIRD consecutive mpox vacuum: only generic BSE healthcare index moves, an insidermonkey small-cap listicle dump (none mpox/GOVX), and macro risk-off (Dow -600, S&P -2.1%) which hits speculative micro-caps hardest. No mpox-specific item, no orders, no GOVX mention. GOVX remains the only known pure-play vehicle. The structural risk I flagged is now acute: a late-tier sentiment name with no fresh catalyst, in a risk-off tape, three weeks into silence — this is exactly the decay setup. Catalyst is still technically pending, so thesis stays live and GOVX remains the vehicle, but conviction is thinning fast.
2024-09-06.exit_advice: Hold but on notice — a third consecutive zero-mpox week means the sentiment fuel is gone; exit if the emergency is formally ended or another vacuum week passes with GOVX bleeding.

2024-09-13.thesis: NuScale SMR reactor approval amid AI datacenter power demand
2024-09-13.exit_case: Cash-burn risk persists with no NuScale design-approval milestone or named deal materializing 16 weeks in.
2024-09-13.assessment: This week's news — Fed rate-cut chatter on cooling inflation, and a Benzinga piece touting power/utility names (CEG, AEP) as the under-the-radar AI-power sector — is macro and sector-theme noise. Neither touches NuScale's larger uprated design approval or a named commercial SMR deployment contract, which is my fixed catalyst. The power-AI theme strengthens the demand backdrop but prices a theme, not my milestone. The 2023 base 50MW certification remains the prior, not the live driver. Prior rallies (ADVANCE Act, PJM auction) were sector tailwinds, not resolution. Cash-burn stays a real risk but resolves nothing — no dilution, default, approval, or signed deal. SMR remains the single clean pure-play. Edge present while the NuScale-specific approval/contract is still pending.
2024-09-13.exit_advice: Hold. The NuScale-specific milestone remains pending; this week's news is sector/macro theme, not resolution.
